results.possibleIdentity:
Robert Fanelli is a physicist and musician who was a member of the faculty at the Department of Physics at Brooklyn College of the City University of New York during the late 1960s and 1970s. He is recognized for his interdisciplinary work bridging the fields of science and the arts, particularly through his association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization founded to facilitate collaborations between artists and engineers. His early academic contributions in physics include co-authoring papers such as "The Impossibility of a Quantum Phase Operator" (1969) and "Energy eigenvalues of a quantum anharmonic oscillator" (1982) with R. E. Struzynski. 

Before focusing on physics, Fanelli had a significant background in music, having studied classical harp and jazz vibraphone, and performing professionally with bands in New York. After teaching graduate physics at Brooklyn College for several years, he eventually integrated his scientific and musical interests, turning to computer music composition. His work in this area utilizes the harmonic series, mathematical algorithms, and computers. Since 1999, he has been affiliated with the Brooklyn College Center for Computer Music (BCCM) as a composer and researcher.

webSearchQueries: "Robert Fanelli" E.A.T. Experiments in Art and Technology
